Briefer News

Today's Chinese government publications, made readable.

Government sources · Events, not analysis · Everything cited

United States China
MAY 28, 2026

Xi hosts Serbia and Pakistan in Beijing.

Today's events

  • Serbia state visit. Xi awarded President Vučić the Friendship Medal and signed 23 cooperation agreements, agreeing to align China's next five-year plan with the “Serbia 2030” strategy.1May 25 · Xinhua
  • Industrial profits jump. Large industrial firms earned 2.44 trillion yuan over January–April, up 18.2 percent, with electronics and non-ferrous-metal makers more than doubling their profits.2May 27 · Stats Bureau
  • Services unhooked from hukou. A State Council directive gives migrant workers and their children equal access to schooling, housing, and insurance in the cities where they actually live.3May · State Council
  • Ten-trillion-yuan ‘six networks.’ Central planners detailed six infrastructure networks for 2026–2030 — water, power, computing power, communications, underground pipes, and logistics — to anchor domestic demand.4May 28 · Xinhua
  • Shenzhou-23 launches. A Long March rocket carried three crew to the Tiangong station, including China's first Hong Kong astronaut — a woman payload specialist.5May 24 · SASAC
Show 4 more events
  • Pakistan visit. Marking 75 years of ties, Xi and Prime Minister Shehbaz pledged a closer “community of shared future” and deeper farm and industrial cooperation.6May 25 · Xinhua
  • Rules for human-like AI. A Qiushi commentary, May 27, explained China's first rulebook for human-like AI services — issued by the internet regulator and four ministries, effective July 15.7May 27 · Qiushi
  • A graying, urban China. The 2025 one-percent population survey counted 1.405 billion people, with 22.9 percent now aged 60 or older and 67.7 percent living in cities.8May 22 · Stats Bureau
  • 100 million vehicles. SAIC became the first Chinese automaker to build and sell 100 million vehicles cumulatively; new-energy models now exceed a third of its sales.9May 28 · Xinhua

This week

  • Critical minerals. State Council Decree 839, the Mineral Resources Law Implementation Regulations, takes effect June 15 and drew repeated coverage this week (May 23 and May 25 dailies). It creates a catalog of strategic minerals subject to output controls, lets the state grant mining rights directly when supply is urgent, and for the first time writes rules for Chinese firms' overseas mining — formalizing state control over the extraction end of supply chains China already leads in processing.3May 15 · State Council
  • Vučić state visit. Xi awarded Serbia's president the Friendship Medal — China's highest honor for foreigners — in Beijing on May 25, signed 23 cooperation documents and pledged to align China's coming five-year plan with Serbia's 2030 strategy (May 26 and May 27 dailies). Serbia is one of China's closest partners in Europe and a key node on the Belt and Road.4May 25 · Qiushi
  • Shanxi mine blast. A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Qinyuan, Shanxi killed 82 (May 23 and May 24 dailies). Xi ordered a strict investigation and a nationwide accident-prevention drive within hours, and Vice Premier Zhang Guoqing was dispatched to the site. It was the week's deadliest single event and the only one to interrupt the leadership's diplomatic schedule.5May 22 · Qiushi
  • Sharif state visit. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif wrapped a four-day visit on May 25; the two sides agreed to accelerate the China–Pakistan Economic Corridor and signed deals on trade and AI (May 25 and May 26 dailies). Xi also thanked Sharif for Pakistan's role mediating U.S.–Iran talks — the week's one direct link between Beijing's diplomacy and the Iran war, then in its 87th day.6May 25 · Qiushi
  • Huawei chip principle. Huawei's semiconductor chief He Tingbo unveiled a new chip-design principle, the ‘τ Law,’ at a Beijing industry conference (May 25 and May 26 dailies). It was China's clearest public claim of progress in designing advanced chips under U.S. export controls — a bid to route around the lithography limits the controls were built to enforce.7May 26 · Xinhua
Read the full digest →

Voices

“The ironclad friendship between China and Serbia stands out as unique, with a deep historical logic and a solid foundation in reality.”

President Xi Jinping · May 251

“This is a war that should never have happened in the first place, and there is no need whatsoever for it to go on.”

MFA Spokesperson Mao Ning · May 2510

“China is firmly opening its financial sector and welcomes financial institutions from all countries, including France, to deepen their roots in the Chinese market.”

PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng · May 2511
Show 3 more voices

“The international community must see clearly through Japan's ‘deceptive diplomacy’ and ‘victim disguise,’ and jointly curb Japan's neo-militarism.”

Defense Ministry Spokesman Sr. Col. Jiang Bin · May 2812

“We must make the service sector a strategic priority for expanding domestic demand, growing the supply of services to better meet rapidly rising demand.”

Shanghai Party Secretary Chen Jining · May 2713

“Artificial intelligence is evolving rapidly, bringing unprecedented opportunities for development — and, with them, risks and challenges never before encountered.”

Qiushi commentary · May 277
Sources
  1. Xinhua (via Supreme People's Procuracy), “Xi Jinping Holds Talks with Serbian President Vučić,” May 25, 2026. spp.gov.cn/…/xi-vucic-talks
  2. National Bureau of Statistics, “Profits of Large Industrial Enterprises Grew 18.2% in January–April 2026,” May 27, 2026. stats.gov.cn/…/industrial-profits-jan-apr
  3. State Council, “Opinions on Providing Basic Public Services at Place of Permanent Residence” (Guofa [2026] No. 11), May 2026. gov.cn/zhengce/…/basic-public-services
  4. Xinhua, “Why the ‘Six Networks’ — The Deeper Logic of the Policy” (National Development and Reform Commission), May 28, 2026. news.cn/fortune/…/six-networks
  5. SASAC / China Aerospace Science and Technology Corp., “Long March 2F Successfully Launches Shenzhou-23 Crewed Spacecraft,” May 26, 2026. sasac.gov.cn/…/shenzhou-23-launch
  6. Xinhua (via Supreme People's Procuracy), “Xi Jinping Meets Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif,” May 25, 2026. spp.gov.cn/…/xi-shehbaz-meeting
  7. Qiushi, “Regulating Human-like AI Interaction Services with ‘Small, Fast, Flexible’ Lawmaking,” May 27, 2026. qstheory.cn/…/ai-interaction-rules
  8. National Bureau of Statistics, “Main Data Bulletin of the 2025 National 1% Population Sample Survey,” May 22, 2026. stats.gov.cn/…/2025-population-survey
  9. Xinhua, “Chinese Automaker Surpasses 100 Million Vehicles for the First Time,” May 28, 2026. news.cn/fortune/…/100-million-vehicles
  10. Ministry of Foreign Affairs, “Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference,” May 25, 2026. mfa.gov.cn/…/mao-ning-may-25
  11. People's Bank of China, “Governor Pan Gongsheng Meets Banque de France Governor Villeroy de Galhau,” May 25, 2026. pbc.gov.cn/…/pan-gongsheng-france
  12. Ministry of National Defense, “Defense Ministry: The International Community Must See Through Japan's ‘Deceptive Diplomacy’ and ‘Victim Disguise,’” May 28, 2026. mod.gov.cn/…/japan-remilitarization
  13. Shanghai Municipal Government, “Shanghai Service Industry Conference: Services as the Strategic Focus of Domestic Demand,” May 28, 2026. shanghai.gov.cn/…/service-industry-conference
